PRE-REVIEW: CR-108 DocuBuilder Genesis Sandbox — RECOMMEND

Verified frontmatter:
- title: "DocuBuilder Genesis Sandbox" — present, non-empty
- revision_summary: "Emphasize exploratory nature, no required outcomes, scope restriction" — present, descriptive

Document structure: All required CR sections (1-12) present. Sections 7.4/7.5 correctly omitted (not a code CR). Testing Summary correctly simplified for non-code CR. Implementation Plan appropriately minimal for exploratory work.

Placeholder check: No author-time {{...}} placeholders remain. All execution-time [...] placeholders correctly present in Sections 10-12. No TBD, TODO, FIXME, or XXX found.

Scope clarity: Well-defined and bounded. Section 5.2 explicitly restricts authorized work to docu-builder/ and the .gitignore entry. Exclusions are enumerated (QMS documents, qms-cli, flow-state, all other files).

Internal consistency: Purpose, Scope, Change Description, Impact Assessment, and EI table are mutually consistent. Files listed in Section 2.3 match Section 7.1 impact table.

Observation (non-blocking): Implementation Plan steps 3-4 (begin prototyping, iterate freely) have no corresponding EIs. The EI table captures only the concrete setup steps plus bookend commits. This is acceptable given the explicit genesis sandbox framing ("no deliverables, no milestones, and no required outcomes" and "The CR may be closed at any time in any state"), but the author should be aware that post-review will verify execution against the EI table, not the Implementation Plan's aspirational steps.

Impact Assessment: Appropriately scoped. No QMS documents affected. No controlled code modified. Sandbox is entirely git-ignored and isolated.

VR flags: No EIs flagged for VR — appropriate for a non-code genesis sandbox.

Formatting: Consistent markdown throughout. No typos or grammatical errors detected.

Traceability: References SOP-001, SOP-002, and originating session artifacts. Parent Document correctly stated as "None."

Review team: No Technical Unit reviewers assigned. This CR does not modify controlled code, UI, or any domain-specific artifacts — QA review alone is sufficient for a genesis sandbox authorization.
